item.title: Vanuatu demands France hand back remote Matthew and Hunter Islands in the Pacific
item.description: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

item.title: Worker accommodation opens in Horsham but more housing needed
item.description: The first stage of a $9 million private worker accommodation development, designed to make it easier to attract essential workers to western Victoria, has reached completion.

item.title: Royal Society of Tasmania turning colonial maritime art album into a digital flip book
item.description: The Royal Society of Tasmania has owned an album of Owen Stanley's maritime artworks since 1900, but its delicate nature meant it could not be put on display. Now, the society is converting the album into a digital flip book so everyone can see it.

item.title: A bassy Viking row and a living statue: The best World Cup fan traditions
item.description: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

item.title: Dutch fans take over US town amid World Cup frenzy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bosnian fans lined up outside World Cup match
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian fashion designers faced with 'roadblocks' fighting fast-fashion duplicates
item.description: Small Australian fashion designers who have had their designs copied by overseas ultra-fast fashion companies say they have hit continuous roadblocks trying to get support.

item.title: Newcastle Knights player Asu Kepaoa banned for six weeks after using homophobic slur
item.description: Newcastle Knights player Asu Kepaoa is handed a six-week ban for using a homophobic slur during a NSW Cup match.

item.title: Showers to chase away the chill of Queensland's winter
item.description: A warm change will arrive in Queensland this weekend, along with scattered rain and warnings of strong winds in the north.

item.title: Stella Magnisalis fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars
item.description: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

item.title: Puppies stolen from Brisbane animal shelter
item.description: Police say two Staffordshire bull terriers were taken from the refuge just before midnight.

item.title: Road train fitted with CT scanner screens remote Northern Territory residents for lung cancer
item.description: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

item.title: How behaviour and biology decide the outbreak equation
item.description: The bugs that cause Ebola, hantavirus and diphtheria are microscopic, but many of the barriers to controlling them are human-sized.

item.title: Developer gets green-light to turn Sydney boarding house into luxury homes after tenants rehoused
item.description: A rundown boarding house which was once a haven for vulnerable people in inner Sydney is set to be converted into luxury apartments.

item.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free two years after last sightings
item.description: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.

item.title: Military-grade thermal imaging and helicopters were deployed to achieve the complete eradication of feral pigs from Australia's third-largest island.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: What the Socceroos need to progress to knockout stage of 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

item.title: 'ISIS bride' on temporary exclusion order issued permit to return home
item.description: Australia's home affairs minister approves the return of a woman who has links to Islamic State and has been in a Syrian refugee camp, saying the government plans to have her under high-level surveillance.

item.title: SA wildlife carers did not expect to be 'ground zero' for Australia's third H5 bird flu case
item.description: A South Australian wildlife rescue group is working with local authorities after Australia's third confirmed case of H5 bird flu was detected in a migratory bird.

item.title: Teals Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall form new political party Community Strong Australia
item.description: The teal independents have confirmed they are forming a new political party, Community Strong Australia, but are yet to convince crossbench colleagues to join what they call a "responsible alternative" to the major parties.

item.title: English cricket captain Ben Stokes speaks for the first time since nightclub incident
item.description: English cricket captain Ben Stokes speaks to media for the first time since he was banned from playing after an incident at a nightclub.

item.title: Decade-long fight for clean drinking water at remote Kimberley community
item.description: Residents of Pandanus Park continue their struggle to access safe drinking water amid a decade-long wait for a solution to dangerous nitrate in their tap supply.

item.title: Olympian arrested at Washington's reflecting pool is Trump 'scapegoat', lawyer says
item.description: A lawyer for the Olympian arrested at Washington's reflecting pool says his client is a scapegoat who was targeted to distract Americans from the Trump administration's failures.

item.title: Powerful earthquakes in Venezuela cause buildings to collapse in capital Caracas
item.description: Two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, local time, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas.

item.title: Venezuela hit by devastating double earthquake — as it happened
item.description: Venezuela's Acting President Delcy Rodríguez says at least 971 have been confirmed injured after emergency services worked through the night to find those trapped under collapsed buildings.

item.title: Buildings collapse in Caracas after earthquakes hit Venezuela
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Private security firm starts patrolling Ballarat streets in response to crime surge
item.description: About 20 residents in the Victorian city of Ballarat are paying a security company to patrol their streets as a "crime deterrent".

item.title: Women launch class action alleging sexual assault and discrimination at mining giant Fortescue Metals Group
item.description: Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest's mining giant Fortescue has been slapped with a landmark class action alleging systemic sexual harassment, discrimination and toxic work sites.

item.title: 'Moist Mondays' help young Australians take the plunge and make new friends
item.description: Young people are turning to local community groups to get out of their comfort zones, make real connections and boost their wellbeing.

item.title: First Nations artists turn ocean waste into sculptures on Cape York Peninsula
item.description: Thousands of tonnes of ocean waste lands on Australia's shores every year. These Queenslanders have found a creative use for some of it.

item.title: Widespread destruction in Venezuela after earthquake
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Employment growth is slowing, despite headline unemployment rate falling
item.description: Australia's unemployment rate slipped to 4.4 per cent in May, down from 4.5 per cent in April, in seasonally adjusted terms.

item.title: Ceiling collapses in Venezuela airport after earthquake
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Brisbane Broncos warn of scammers offering fake NRL mascot auditions
item.description: A scam is circulating online falsely offering fans a chance to audition to be the Brisbane Broncos' mascot in exchange for payment.

item.title: Public understanding of harmful algal blooms still low, survey finds
item.description: Most Australians remain uncertain about the cause of harmful algal blooms and are more worried about disasters such as bushfires and flooding.

item.title: Ceiling collapses in Venezuela airport after earthquake
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: AEMO expects more batteries and less transmission in the grid of tomorrow
item.description: Home batteries hit 600,000 as households play big role in energy transition, but building transmission projects remains "critical", according to the latest AEMO energy plan.

item.title: How Karl Stefanovic went from Today show glory to 'Joe Bogan' podcaster
item.description: Today show host Karl Stefanovic is reportedly leaving Channel Nine after a more than 20-year career at the network that was filled with highs and lows.

item.title: Councils deny EnergyCo town water for New England REZ construction
item.description: Rural NSW councils already under water restrictions are drawing a line in the sand regarding supplying water to Renewable Energy Zone projects.

item.title: Kalbarri braces for economic shock as Chris Ellison's MinRes shutters Lucky Bay garnet mine
item.description: Mineral Resources's decision to close the Lucky Bay Garnet Mine will see 110 jobs lost in and around Kalbarri.

item.title: Joshua Wishart jailed for fatal attack on Hervey Bay unit manager Alfred Westmoreland
item.description: A West Australian man has been jailed for fatally striking a 75-year-old man who stepped in to protect his wife and a sex worker during an altercation at a Queensland apartment complex.

item.title: Power price rise on the way for Tasmanian households, businesses with Aurora Energy
item.description: Tasmanian households and businesses with the state-owned electricity retailer will be paying more for power, after a price rise was approved by the economic regulator.

item.title: Verified videos show impact of earthquakes in Venezuela
item.description: ABC NEWS Verify has authenticated videos showing the devastation after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's coast.

item.title: What today's World Cup results mean for each team that played, as Scotland's hopes fade
item.description: The path to the World Cup knockouts is more complicated than ever. Here is what Thursday's results mean for the path ahead.

item.title: Long lunches and sickies on the cards as Australia prepares to take on Paraguay in World Cup match
item.description: The countdown is on for Australia's World Cup match against Paraguay, and some football fans are preparing to happily put work on the backburner.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Harry Kane's curse lifted, historic goals for Haiti, Guillermo Ochoa gets his moment
item.description: A Ghanaian witch doctor releases a curse on England striker Harry Kane, Haiti scores its first World Cup goals in 52 years and 40-year-old Mexico keeper Guillermo Ochoa makes an emotional appearance from the bench. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: World Cup: Tony Popovic has a selection headache. But that's a good thing
item.description: Tony Popovic dances to his own selection tune, but that doesn't mean there aren't loud calls for him to select Australia's most attacking weapons against Paraguay.

item.title: Podcaster Tobias Nuttall jailed for life for murder of girlfriend in her Perth home
item.description: Tobias Nuttall is sentenced to at least 18 years behind bars for murdering his girlfriend with a 25-centimetre dagger in a "vicious" attack in her suburban Perth home.

item.title: Buildings destroyed in La Guaira in Venezuela
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hanson's increased visibility comes at a price for One Nation
item.description: Parliament House has been Pauline's World lately but increased visibility comes with increased scrutiny and this week revealed that One Nation is not untouchable.

item.title: SA council elections delayed until April 2027 after legislation passes parliament
item.description: The state government has passed legislation to delay South Australia's council elections until April 2027, after the Electoral Commission raised "very serious concerns" about its ability to administer the poll in November.

item.title: Questions over missed 'warning signs' after Chinese language school closes abruptly
item.description: The shock closure of a Chinese language school after 17 years leaves parents and teachers with "unanswered questions" over the school's financial management.

item.title: Concerns 72yo man granted bail after alleged 'offensive' graffiti spree could endanger community safety
item.description: A 72-year-old man arrested over dozens of alleged graffiti and malicious damage offences in Sydney's inner west has been granted bail, after a judge expressed surprise that he was not charged under NSW hate speech laws.

item.title: Former AFL umpire Michael Pell to stand trial over alleged Brownlow betting fraud
item.description: Ex-AFL umpire Michael Pell, accused of leaking information on confidential Brownlow votes, has been ordered to stand trial.

item.title: Government and union endorse 16 per cent pay rise offer for SA nurses and midwives
item.description: Nurses and midwives in South Australia would receive a 16 per cent pay rise by mid-2028 under a new deal endorsed by their union and the state government.

item.title: Farmers claim partial victory after Queensland government backflips on gas
item.description: Farmers are claiming a partial victory after the Queensland government backed down on controversial changes to gas mining approvals above a vital underground water source.

item.title: Eels coach punches a wall and bemoans 'park football' effort
item.description: Parramatta coach Jason Ryles has said his side's performance on Thursday night was not good enough to "beat a park football", going down 32-12 to the South Sydney Rabbitohs.

item.title: Labor eyes tougher laws to enforce social media ban for children
item.description: Australia will strengthen its social media ban for young people as a priority amid concerns the powers behind the world-first laws are not strong enough.

item.title: Alex Antic's 'dangerous' comments on vaccinations after bird flu detection condemned
item.description: South Australian Liberal senator Alex Antic has been roundly condemned over comments he made about vaccinations after avian influenza was found on Australia's southern coast.

item.title: Venezuelan Kevin Lugo on rush to contact family in Caracas after earthquakes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route, restricts flights between Darwin and Singapore to peak season
item.description: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

item.title: ACT extends Indigenous Circle Sentencing in Supreme Court after successful pilot program
item.description: A pilot program allowing First Nations offenders to be sentenced by magistrates sitting alongside elders in the ACT Supreme Court secures funding for another three years.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Lions roar back in form with big win over Swans
item.description: The defending champions, Brisbane, make a statement with a big win over second-placed Sydney at the Gabba.

item.title: Earthquake hits Caracas shopping mall
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Powerful earthquakes strike Venezuela
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: The deadly nitrous oxide flooding into Australia
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Is the alliance between Trump and Netanyahu over?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Socceroos prepare for their big game
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Destroyed buildings in La Guaira, Venezuela after earthquakes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Thousands feared dead as major rescue operation underway in earthquake-struck Venezuela
item.description: Jorge Rodríguez, who heads Venezuela's national assembly, says at least 235 people have died, 1,520 people have been injured, and at least 250 buildings have been damaged or destroyed.

item.title: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has claimed hundreds of lives, health authorities say
item.description: At least 101 million Europeans were forecast to swelter in temperatures over 35 degrees on Thursday, with hundreds of people believed to have been killed by the heatwave.

item.title: ABC news quiz: Teals have a party, Neil the Seal does battle and Trump drains his swamp
item.description: This week's news quiz features the usual assortment of political animals and regular animals, as well as other odd happenings from across the world.

item.title: Friend-making events are popping up everywhere – this is who you might meet
item.description: From recently single, to expats, paid and free clubs designed to help adults make friends are attracting people from all walks of life. As Australians increasingly report feeling lonely, these are the people who are branching out to change that.

item.title: The 20 best albums of 2026 so far, from RAYE, Kacey Musgraves and more
item.description: If you haven't had the time to keep up with new music this year, we've got you covered. Find your new favourite album here.

item.title: High Court risk if Islamic State-linked woman barred from Australia, expert warns
item.description: Legal experts say the Commonwealth would have risked losing a High Court challenge to counterterrorism laws if it stopped the Australian woman from returning home.

item.title: Sexual misconduct findings not referred for Victorian Working with Children Check review
item.description: Victoria's former children's commissioner didn't refer sexual misconduct cases for a Working with Children Check review, despite saying she would never have done so in cases involving any sexual element "whatsoever".

item.title: What can Australia learn from Asia's battle with H5N1 variant bird flu?
item.description: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

item.title: Paraguay's Australian-made commune nervous for crucial Cup clash
item.description: In rural Paraguay, a small farming town flies Australian flags. When Paraguay plays Australia at the 2026 World Cup today, its residents will be at the edge of their seats.

item.title: Neil the seal not immune from new bird flu threat despite isolated lifestyle
item.description: Experts say it is a matter of when, not if, a deadly strain of bird flu reaches Tasmania, and popular marine visitor Neil the seal is not isolated from the threat, despite his relatively solo lifestyle.

item.title: Sweden screamer and Ecuador's winner deliver Socceroos blow
item.description: Ecuador beats Germany 2-1 to almost certainly seal progression to the next round, while Côte d'Ivoire wraps up second spot in Group E with a win over Curaçao. Follow live.

item.title: The Venezuelan port town of La Guaira was badly hit by the earthquakes.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Thriving Kids program not intended to be state-based, advisory panel member claims
item.description: A member of the Thriving Kids advisory panel has voiced concern about how the support program for children with autism and developmental delay is being rolled out.

item.title: Matthew Guy says he's owed apology over Moira Deeming assault allegation
item.description: Victoria Police close an investigation into allegations state MP Matthew Guy assaulted fellow Liberal Moira Deeming at a community event, determining there was "there was no offence detected".

item.title: Tele-oncology units at West Moreton hospitals cut cancer patients' travel time
item.description: Cancer patients in Brisbane's rural outskirts have saved thousands of kilometres of travel to access oncology services.

item.title: Today show host Karl Stefanovic to leave Channel Nine, network confirms
item.description: Today show host Karl Stefanovic will leave Channel Nine after a controversial podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson.

item.title: Rescue organisations say they need clear guidelines as vets turn away wild birds
item.description: Wildlife rescuers say they need help to prepare for a potential bird flu outbreak as vets issue statements saying they can no longer take in sick or injured wild birds.

item.title: Death toll rises to at least 235 as major rescue operation continues — as it happened
item.description: More than 250 buildings have been lost or damaged after the devastating double quake, National Assembly president Jorge Rodríguez says.

item.title: Influx of over-50s lifestyle communities on Capricorn Coast prompts mixed reaction
item.description: Four over-50s lifestyle developments have been approved for this small beach community, with two more being assessed. Some worry the area is not ready for the influx, others say it is the answer to the housing crisis.

item.title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla have no plans to move into renovated Buckingham Palace
item.description: An annual financial statement released on Thursday contained several revelations, including plans to cut the royal family's public funding, and details of how much tax the monarch paid.

item.title: What time do the Socceroos play today and how can you watch Australia vs Paraguay live?
item.description: The Socceroos can make it through to the knockout stage of the World Cup against Paraguay today. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Veterans call for $5,000 allied health cap to be removed
item.description: An Australian Defence Force veteran says caps on allied health spending for veterans are a betrayal and that any politician supportive of the changes would not be welcome at Anzac Day events.

item.title: Red Cross shares rescue video after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Jetski riders rescue 'Princess Peach' the turtle stranded on Magnetic Island beach
item.description: A group of jetski riders were touring Magnetic Island when they stumbled across an injured turtle.

item.title: Abalone divers fuming as government slashes catch amounts by 41 per cent
item.description: The NSW government says a reset is needed to ensure the sustainable future of the abalone fishery along the south coast, but commercial divers say the reduction is a "pretty disgusting state of affairs".

item.title: Former Socceroos coach Frank Farina understands pressure of Paraguay clash
item.description: Former national team coach Frank Farina will be among the many Australians who will be tuning in to the Socceroos' decisive clash with Paraguay today.

item.title: Australian Dane Sweeny makes his first overseas grand slam in Wimbledon qualifiers
item.description: Australia's Dane Sweeny makes it through to an overseas grand slam for the first time after a hard-fought victory in the Wimbledon qualifiers.

item.title: Families struggle to reach loved ones in Venezuela
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Building collapses after earthquakes rock Venezuela
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bunbury to pay $1.2 million a year to truck organic waste 160km to Perth
item.description: Bunbury ratepayers are set to foot the bill in a bid to resurrect Western Australia's three-bin FOGO waste management system from collapse outside of Perth.

item.title: Supergirl deserves better than a watered-down action movie
item.description: Australian Milly Alcock shines as Supergirl in the hero's first solo outing since 1984. If only the rest of the movie sparkled around her.

item.title: World Cup third place table: Which teams will make it to the knockouts?
item.description: The World Cup group stage is winding down, with third-placed teams anxiously watching other results to learn their fate. See the live table to see who is on track to progress.

item.title: WA farming town turns to 3D printing to house its migrant workers
item.description: With a wealth of job opportunities but no houses for skilled workers, a regional WA town is hoping 3D-printed homes can solve the shortage.

item.title: Socceroos seal World Cup progression with gritty draw against Paraguay
item.description: The Socceroos are through to the World Cup round of 32 thanks to a tense and physical 0-0 draw with Paraguay in San Francisco.

item.title: Helicopter flies over earthquake-affected areas in Venezuela
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: People and pets' lives at risk if fire ants not eradicated, study finds
item.description: If Australia does not ramp up fire ant eradication efforts, the invasive species will cause billion-dollar economic impacts and dozens of deaths, a study finds.

item.title: Queensland man still chasing sailing dreams despite terminal illness
item.description: David Burston had only just bought an old yacht to fix up and sail around Australia when he was diagnosed with stage 4 cancer. Hee has decided to keep pursuing his dream for as long as he can.

item.title: India skipper says team ready to take down Australia in women's T20 World Cup clash at Lord's
item.description: India's skipper Harmanpreet Kaur says her team has learnt plenty of lessons as it prepares to face Australia in a crucial group game at the women's T20 World Cup final.

item.title: Daughter released without charge after mother's body found at Western Sydney aged care facility
item.description: Police have released a 53-year-old woman in Sydney's west after a substance was allegedly administered to her mother, who was found dead in an aged care home.

item.title: Karl Stefanovic's move to outsider politics follows a well-worn lucrative path
item.description: Listening to the culture war showman schtick he has so quickly adopted, Karl Stefanovic is now less a newsman than an ideological performer.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Ecuador does the impossible, Sweden trains the house down
item.description: Ecuador's wildest dreams come true, the Swedes get a rude shock at training and a piece of World Cup history goes up for sale. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Why the Dolphins clash with the Warriors is the NRL's unlikeliest blockbuster
item.description: In a season bereft of quality, the Dolphins and the Warriors will meet in a marquee clash that doubles as the NRL's most unusual big game showdown.

item.title: Concerns China's new ethnic unity law could put Australian citizens at risk
item.description: The Australian government raises concerns directly with China over the country's new ethnic unity law, which allows Beijing to target people overseas.

item.title: Queensland to enforce harsher penalties on drink and drug driving laws from December
item.description: The Crisafulli government has announced a crackdown on dangerous driving while doubling down on a "zero-tolerance" approach to drug driving, including for medical cannabis users.

item.title: The two earthquakes that struck Venezuela are known as a 'doublet'. Here's what that is
item.description: The deadly one-two punch that toppled buildings in Venezuela's capital and beyond was a doublet earthquake, a pair of similar-sized quakes close in location and time.

item.title: Disunity but not death for Basil Zempilas and WA Liberals after MP quits
item.description: Basil Zempilas has had his first brush with the political truism "disunity is death" after MP Jonathan Huston quit the WA Liberal Party, but he's unlikely — at this stage at least — to be the one left in a political grave.

item.title: Why the live action Moana has come so soon after the 2024 sequel, original animated movie
item.description: Dwayne Johnson and Sydneysider Catherine Lagaʻaia have walked the blue carpet at a special event in Sydney. They told ABC News why now is the perfect time for a live action Moana.

item.title: Tsunami the dog deployed in Venezuela earthquakes rescue mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: When do the Socceroos play next in the World Cup and who will Australia play?
item.description: Australia has secured its placed in the knockout stages of the World Cup — but who will they play and when?

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos through to FIFA World Cup round of 32, but goals still hard to find
item.description: The Socceroos are into the knockout rounds of the World Cup, but they still have a big problem to solve.

item.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA
item.description: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

item.title: Will bird flu in Australia affect the price of eggs?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Tony Popovic gives Socceroos fans what they want, Jordy Bos and Jackson Irvine star as Australia progresses
item.description: Tony Popovic gives Socceroos fans what they wanted, fan sites overflow as a "headache" epidemic sweeps schools, and Australia avoids the most nervous of waits. Here are the quick hits from the Socceroos' final group game against Paraguay.

item.title: How I save money by storing and cooking things differently
item.description: I've developed simple storage and cooking systems that keep food visible and fresh while ensuring I use things up. Here's how I make cooking for one feel more doable, affordable and lower-impact.

item.title: How Australians across the country watched the Socceroos and Paraguay World Cup match
item.description: Crowds across the nation adorned in green and gold gathered in support of the Socceroos for their third match in the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: WaterNSW's price hike worry farmers as costs increase 40 per cent for some
item.description: WaterNSW in a "death spiral" and irrigators say steep price rises for water will not save it.

item.title: Operators cancel cuttlefish season resulting in million-dollar losses
item.description: Whyalla has been forced to wind back services and let staff let go as its tourism industry suffers million-dollar losses from cuttlefish not making their usual migration.

item.title: Essendon announces selection panel to pick next AFL coach
item.description: Essendon has opted to go in-house with the panel that will decide whether James Hird returns as its AFL senior coach.

item.title: Voice head calls for more funding while demonstrating ongoing success
item.description: South Australia's First Nations Voice to Parliament reforms are being revealed.

item.title: Echuca Moama Taxi Group fined over deaths of two passengers in wheelchairs
item.description: A Victorian taxi company has been fined over the separate 2021 deaths of two passengers who used wheelchairs.

item.title: Youth offender program provider Namu Collective referred to authorities over alleged misappropriation of public funds
item.description: Namu Collective was awarded a contract last year for the government's "staying on track" program to stop youth offenders from falling back into the cycle of crime.

item.title: Broncos coach rejects claim season is done despite seventh straight loss
item.description: Queensland halfback Sam Walker is put on report for a shoulder charge in the Roosters' 24-18 win over Brisbane, potentially ruling him out of State of Origin III.

item.title: Judge finds mental health issues spurred grandmother's roadside stabbing attack
item.description: A 60-year-old woman who stabbed her grandson and daughter-in-law has been sentenced to jail. Her defence team had argued she was suffering sudden and extreme anger partially induced by weight-loss medication.

item.title: Canberra wildlife sanctuary builds quarantine pens amid fears H5 bird flu could 'decimate' quoll population
item.description: A population of endangered eastern quolls is being protected by a sanctuary in Canberra, but the threat of bird flu could mean that protection does not go far enough.

item.title: Menopause and perimenopause support expands into nationwide clinics
item.description: Access to physiotherapy, dietetics and psychology services will become routine in a national expansion to menopause and perimenopause support from July 1.

item.title: Andrew 'Twiggy' Forrest warns Fortescue staff after class action alleges sexual assault
item.description: In an email sent to all staff, Mr Forrest said: "Anyone who thinks this behaviour has a place at Fortescue is in the wrong company."

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Socceroos players praise Jordy Bos after starring role against Paraguay
item.description: Jordy Bos may not have been named player of the match, but another strong performance, in which he was asked to play on the right of defence instead of his preferred left, was lauded by his teammates.

item.title: Rainbands head for southeast Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Star Hawk hurt as Giants count the injury cost of stinging loss
item.description: The Hawks hold on at the MCG to beat GWS and lock up third spot for the moment, in a game marred by a number of big injuries.

item.title: Ukraine launches heavy drone attack on Russia, annexed Crimea declares 'emergency'
item.description: Authorities in Russian-annexed Crimea have declared an "emergency situation".

item.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building, authorities seal off roads
item.description: Two bystanders have said an aircraft about the size of a car has crashed into Beijing's CITIC Tower.

item.title: US strikes Iran after Trump calls attack in Strait of Hormuz a 'foolish' ceasefire breach
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards vows a "swift and decisive" response, according to Iranian state television, claiming they repelled a US attack on Sirik Island, on the shores of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Live: Australia's next opponent changes multiple times in World Cup chaos
item.description: All four teams in Group G could play Australia in the knockout stage, with multiple goals changing the picture several times already. Follow live.

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Bluey's friends, a glam rock classic and where to look for rainbows
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Venue merch cuts are still putting artists at a loss, what needs to change?
item.description: Three years after a defining flashpoint in the dispute over merchandise cuts, venues and artists are still at an impasse.

item.title: Search for love takes singles off the apps and into the bush for old-school romance
item.description: Forget swiping right, singles are being urged to head to the bush in their search for real-life romance.

item.title: In Venezuela, the earthquake started with a 'soft shake' and then the lights went out
item.description: As a rare one-two punch of earthquakes hit Venezuela this week, residents in the capital Caracas watched in horror as buildings shook and were sent "pancaking" before their eyes.

item.title: Government to shake up to home loan subsidy scheme for Defence force members and veterans
item.description: Critics say the scheme, which offers subsidised home loans, lumps Defence members and veterans with uncompetitive rates.

item.title: Vial of unapproved peptide retatrutide had double the strength indicated on label
item.description: Doctors warn of an "enormous" risk of overdose after testing of a popular unapproved weight loss peptide reveals the product had double the strength of what was listed on its label.

item.title: How on earth has the global economy survived this ongoing war?
item.description: An unfortunate side effect of the incompetent and erratic negotiating tactics of the Trump administration is that some of the underlying stories in the global economy can get overlooked.

item.title: The Interview: John Williamson on what it means to be True Blue in 2026
item.description: Now that folk and country music legend John Williamson is 80, he wants to spend less time touring and more time at home eating his own tomatoes.

item.title: Water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre 'best' locals have seen
item.description: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

item.title: The deadly H5 bird flu is here. These numbers show the devastation overseas
item.description: The deadly H5 bird flu has finally hit Australia. Here's how other nations have coped with the virus.

item.title: Can David Farley deliver on One Nation's promises after Farrer residents voted for change?
item.description: Farrer residents decisively voted to change their once-safe seat. They are now putting Pauline Hanson and One Nation to the test.

item.title: Family urges Voluntary Assisted Dying law reform to give dementia patients right to die
item.description: Families and dementia patients say they should be able to die on their own terms, so why isn't dementia treated like other incurable diseases? What would it take to change Voluntary Assisted Dying laws?

item.title: Football helps migrants on their settlement journey and children 'reclaim their joy'
item.description: On a crisp winter's evening at Fairfield in Western Sydney, a common passion warms a group of footballers braving the night air.

item.title: Beer follows wine industry's lead with terroir-driven ales
item.description: A boutique South Australian malt house is producing flavour-focused malt from barley grown in different regions.

item.title: H5N1 bird flu a deadly threat to native duck species already menaced from all angles
item.description: Australia's native duck species were already threatened by urban sprawl, climate change, hunting and habitat degradation. Now H5N1 bird flu has been discovered on our shores.

item.title: Tony Popovic has made some surprising selection calls at the World Cup, but he's not going to discuss his reasons
item.description: When the Socceroos team to face Paraguay was named, there were a record six changes from the side that lost to the USA. But Tony Popovic won't explain why.

item.title: Hikers lost in Kosciuszko National Park rescued by drones powered by AI
item.description: Rescuers were able to locate the hikers less than five hours after they were reported missing.

item.title: Winter weather and El Niño on backburner as tropical humidity arrives to soak Australia
item.description: For an El Niño year, 2026 is so far the wettest on record for Australia, and an influx of tropical moisture is about to deliver another round of widespread rain.

item.title: Jet fuel price drops won't mean cheaper airfares — at least for now
item.description: Airlines raised prices for flights in response to high fuel costs but experts say there will be a lag in bringing airfares back down.

item.title: Why a remote north Qld boat ramp became the centre of an international drug seizure investigation
item.description: Inside the journey of how the drugs from Australia's biggest seizure reached our shores.

item.title: Can the Victorian Liberal Party avoid falling apart again?
item.description: The Victorian Liberal Party is competitive in the polls just months out from the November state election. But will party infighting blunt its winning edge?

item.title: Study results prove First Nations midwifery program leads to healthier babies
item.description: First Nations babies born through a midwifery program offered at three Melbourne hospitals are more likely to be full term and a healthy weight, a study has found.

item.title: Olive growers excited as consumer demand rises, industry looks to expand
item.description: Consumer interest in olive oil has boomed at the same time as growing the produce has flourished, leading to excitement in the industry.

item.title: Queensland conservationists brace for potential bird flu battle
item.description: Home to the highest number of bird species in Australia, the sunshine state could have the most to lose from a bird flu outbreak.

item.title: Experts warn the polyphagous shot-hole borer could wreak havoc on Brisbane's parks if it reaches the east coast
item.description: A sesame seed-sized pest has destroyed 4,800 trees in Perth. Experts warn Brisbane's hot climate and dense tree population could be the perfect breeding ground for the pest.

item.title: Australian Maya Joint drawn to play Serena Williams in first round of Wimbledon, Alex de Minaur gets Roman Andres Burruchag
item.description: Australia's Maya Joint will take centre stage at Wimbledon after she was drawn to face Serena Williams in the opening round.

item.title: NSW budget another 'missed opportunity' for First Nations communities
item.description: There have been three state budgets since the failed Voice referendum in October 2023, and this year marked the fourth for the Minns Labor government.

item.title: Art 'like medicine' for accountant turned painter after brain injury
item.description: After undergoing brain surgery, Cheryl Blacklock can no longer process numbers quickly and has instead found a new life through art, painting faces that appear as visions in her mind.

item.title: Virginia Trioli's final column: 27 things I've learned at the ABC
item.description: After almost 40 years in Australian journalism, 27 of them at the ABC, I am now in possession of enough mistakes to claim a small amount of experience.

item.title: Meet CJ Jones: The 17-year-old Tasmanian who stole audiences' hearts in The Piano
item.description: The Piano taught CJ Jones about friendship and perseverance. He’s now set for his debut on the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra concert stage at the ABC Classic 100 in Concert.

item.title: Dung beetle exchange between South Australia and Tasmania ensures manure is managed all year round
item.description: While some dung beetles rest, others get to work, and farmers are making trades.

item.title: Ireland stuns India with historic 34-run T20 international win in Dublin
item.description: Ireland records its first ever international victory over India with a 34-run T20 win in Dublin.

item.title: NT Labor MLA Manuel Brown’s driving infringements further fuel party turmoil
item.description: Revelations about an NT shadow minister’s dicey driving history — and the Opposition Leader's failure to disclose it — come as another blow to NT Labor.

item.title: John didn't realise he had pancreatic cancer until he taught a workshop on the disease. This is his message
item.description: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

item.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is the wonderkid dubbed God's gift to Indian cricket
item.description: On tour with India and on the brink of his international debut, the pressure is being heaped on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

item.title: Double brick dominates Perth homes. Experts say it could be slowing down construction
item.description: It takes 12 to 18 months on average to build a home in Western Australia — but with non-brick frame construction, the home building timeline could be cut by up to half.

item.title: World Cup: Ousmane Dembélé suspends time as France's favouritism heightens
item.description: Ousmane Dembélé showed patience and a command of time and space, to help propel France to the top of its group, and remain on course for World Cup glory.

item.title: Portrait exhibition showcases stories of southern WA's Menang elders
item.description: A photography exhibition in Albany shares the stories of Menang elders, who often have been overlooked in history.

item.title: How boxer Hussein Dehaini became the latest casualty of Melbourne's underworld conflict
item.description: Those who knew Hussein Dehaini say he could have been anything he wanted to be. But his life ended at 33, when he was gunned down in the middle of a Melbourne street.

item.title: World Cup: Aziz Behich says he's 'gutted' for Matt Leckie, who has left the Socceroos camp with injury
item.description: Australia enters the World Cup knockout rounds with just 24 players in the squad after veteran Matthew Leckie and right-back Jacob Italiano departed the camp with injuries.

item.title: Polygamous sect leader Samuel Bateman convicted after girls found in trailer on US highway
item.description: A polygamous sect leader already serving a 50-year prison sentence in the US has been convicted of child abuse after girls were found in an unventilated trailer he was hauling through Arizona.

item.title: Rainbow flags wave in Seattle after Egypt and Iran pushed back against World Cup 'Pride Match'
item.description: Rainbow flags wave around Seattle after Egypt and Iran asked FIFA to keep a Pride celebration out of its World Cup match.

item.title: A warm and comforting gnocchi bake - ABC News
item.description: "This would be something I'd love to make on a Sunday and have it bubbling away while you're doing odd jobs around the house."

item.title: Witness captures aircraft fragments and building damage in Beijing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Couple welcomes refugees to home 'like family' in resettlement program
item.description: Margaret River residents Anne and John Roberts's motto is to offer a "soft landing" and a "warm embrace" to refugees as they settle into life in Australia.

item.title: Sacred Ashura tradition a show of 'resistance' in Beirut amidst Israel-Hezbollah conflict
item.description: Tens of thousands of Shiite Muslims gathered in Beirut for the largest Ashura procession in years, as Hezbollah used the holy day to project strength despite growing pressure to disarm after months of war with Israel.

item.title: Attempted copper theft from Energex leaves 13,000 south-east Queensland customers without power
item.description: Energex says it appears someone broke into a substation's cable pit and attempted to cut through an 11,000-volt cable, leaving about 13,000 south-east Queensland customers without power overnight.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Bedlam in Group H, Dua Lipa surprise and superstars warm the bench
item.description: Group H delivers us incredible drama, some of the World Cup's biggest stars find themselves warming the pine, and Dua Lipa makes a surprise appearance. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Live: Brilliant Talor Byrne goal sends Carlton well clear ahead of Showdown 60
item.description: Carlton tries to make it six straight wins under interim coach Josh Fraser as they host West Coast, ahead of the 60th edition of the Showdown in Adelaide tonight. Follow live.

item.title: Interactive exhibition encourages patrons to colour in art themselves
item.description: An art studio in Ballarat is hosting an interactive exhibition around a colouring book and visitors can contribute to the art.

item.title: Pictures reveal heartbreaking scenes emerging from earthquakes in Venezuela
item.description: After two powerful earthquakes, rescue teams from around the globe are in a race against time to pull survivors from the rubble.

item.title: Rich and famous families teach their kids Mandarin as China's influence grows
item.description: Rich and powerful families have a strong interest in one particular language, with experts saying it is no surprise given the country's rise.

item.title: Darwin vacation care worker charged with indecently touching, grooming child under 16
item.description: NT Police allege the male educator, 40, indecently touched a child under the age of 16 at a vacation care program in Darwin.

item.title: Cabo Verde's run to World Cup knockout stage has them believing in impossible
item.description: Cabo Verde has secured the most unlikely spot in the World Cup knockouts, and the nation of 500,000 people is daring to dream.

item.title: Pete Buttigieg victim of false anonymous report alleging he was a 'danger' to his children
item.description: High profile Democrat Pete Buttigieg has been the victim of a false anonymous report, alleging he was a 'danger' to his four-year-old twins.

item.title: Live: Queensland trio avoid suspension to be cleared for Origin decider
item.description: The Dolphins and Warriors meet in a blockbuster NRL clash between two genuine premiership contenders at Lang Park. Follow live.
